meta.contract: Every blank is written as {role:shape}. shape 'np' = noun phrase, 'cl' = full clause (subject + verb). The engine performs a lookup only — no grammatical guessing. Question files must supply every role in every shape its essayType's cards request. Fixed 'glue' language (hooks, discourse markers, thesis/close starters) is wrapped in ... so the UI can highlight it; blanks are left unwrapped so filled content stays plain.
meta.source: All 36 templates (6 essay types x 6 variants) transcribed from INTUITY-one-card-models.pdf, © Majid Nashtai 2026. Card ids (FA/PS/CE/AD/OP/ST) match the printed set so on-screen numbers match the physical cards.

rolesByType.for-against: topic (the issue), counter (opposing consideration), pro1/pro2 (arguments for), con1 (argument against), verdict (closing recommendation, only used by FA-3/FA-5/FA-6)
rolesByType.problem-solution: topic (the problem), cause (reason/context for the problem — shape depends on connector: np after 'in order to/owing to', cl after 'due to the fact that/as a result'), sol1/sol2/sol3 (solutions — cl after 'is that', np after 'to' or 'tackled by')
rolesByType.cause-effect: topic (the phenomenon), cause (context clause in the open — np after 'due to/owing to', cl after 'given that'), c1/c2/c3 (individual causes — cl after 'is that', np after 'brought about by')
rolesByType.agree-disagree: topic (the statement's subject), counter (the opposing view), verdict (the writer's position — cl after 'agree that', np before 'is, on balance, true'), r1/r2/r3 (personal reasons)
rolesByType.opinion: topic (the subject), context (background clause), r1/r2/r3 (personal reasons), verdict (closing personal conclusion)
rolesByType.speculation-trends: topic (the trend/subject), context (background — np after 'with', cl after 'given that/since'), p1/p2/p3 (predicted developments)
